title: Collections
intro: This page gathers the project's material by theme, in one place: town records, Revolution and occupation, Sagtikos Manor, maps, people, daily life, schools and congregations, race and belonging, and flags.
Each entry links to where the material already lives — a source, an essay, a timeline moment, or a person's page — so you can start from a topic instead of a page name.
provisionalNote: This is an early gathering place, not a finished catalog. Groupings will be refined, and new collections will appear as more of the archive is digitized and reviewed.

townRecords.title: Town Records
townRecords.blurb: The town's first minute book: annual meetings, poor relief, bay rules, wartime pages, and the 1784 turn to the State of New York.

revolution.title: Revolution & Occupation
revolution.blurb: How the Revolution reached Islip: the 1775 pledge, the militia, six years of occupation, raids, and survival.

sagtikos.title: Sagtikos Manor & the Thompsons
sagtikos.blurb: One house at the center of the story: family, offices, occupation, labor, and a presidential visit.

mapsPlaces.title: Maps & Places
mapsPlaces.blurb: The town's shape on paper and on the ground: province maps, necks and creeks, the South Country Road, and the bay.

people.title: People
people.blurb: The lives the archive can name so far, from Winnaquaheagh to Samuel Sitko.

dailyLife.title: Daily Life
dailyLife.blurb: Hogs and fences, fishing rules, songs, sickness, and neighborly care — the everyday world behind the big events.

schoolsChurches.title: Schools, Churches & Community
schoolsChurches.blurb: Schoolhouses and commissioners, congregations, and the institutions communities built to belong.

raceStatus.title: Race, Status & Households
raceStatus.blurb: Who actually lived in early Islip: the 1798 count of households, named lives in the records, and the questions that remain.

flagsSymbols.title: Flags & Symbols
flagsSymbols.blurb: What flew over Islip, what its militia likely marched under, and the seal the town still uses.
